name: Nine-Patch Baby Quilt
description: Nine squares, endless charm. Quick enough to finish before the baby arrives. A great beginner project with satisfying results.
summary: Strip-piece 12 nine-patch blocks and alternate them with solid squares for a classic checkerboard baby quilt. Simple enough for a first project, charming enough to become an heirloom.

materials.name: Lightweight batting
materials.notes: Use a low-loft batting for a baby quilt — it stays soft

steps.title: Make Strip Set A
steps.instruction: Sew one Print A strip, one white strip, and one Print A strip together along the long edges, RST (Right Sides Together). Press seams toward the print. Make 2 of Strip Set A.

tips.text: Alternate sewing direction on each strip to prevent bowing. Sew strip 1 left-to-right, strip 2 right-to-left.

steps.title: Make Strip Set B
steps.instruction: Sew one white strip, one Print B strip, and one white strip together along the long edges, RST. Press seams toward the print. Make 2 of Strip Set B.

tips.text: Use the same seam allowance and pressing direction for all strip sets — consistency is everything.

steps.title: Sub-Cut Strip Sets
steps.instruction: Sub-cut Strip Set A into 3 1/2" segments (you need 24 A segments). Sub-cut Strip Set B into 3 1/2" segments (you need 12 B segments). Use your ruler to keep cuts perpendicular to the seams.

warnings.message: Square up the selvage end before cutting segments. A crooked first cut makes every segment off.

tips.text: You should get about 11-12 segments per strip set, so 2 of each gives you plenty.

steps.title: Assemble Nine-Patch Blocks
steps.instruction: Arrange segments: A-B-A (print corners, white center). Sew the three segments together, nesting seams at intersections. Press. Each block should measure 9 1/2" x 9 1/2" unfinished. Make 12 blocks.

warnings.message: Nest the seams — since Strip Set A and B are pressed in opposite directions, seams should lock together perfectly.

tips.text: Chain-piece all 12 blocks at once: feed all first pairs through, then all second pairs.

steps.title: Arrange and Sew Quilt Top
steps.instruction: Lay out blocks in 5 rows: alternate nine-patch blocks with solid white 9 1/2" squares in a checkerboard. Row 1: nine-patch, white, nine-patch, white (4 blocks). Row 2: white, nine-patch, white, nine-patch. Continue for 5 rows. Sew blocks into rows, pressing seams toward white squares. Join rows, nesting seams.

tips.text: Take a photo of your layout before sewing so you don't lose your arrangement.

steps.title: Quilt and Bind
steps.instruction: Layer flannel backing (wrong side up), batting, and quilt top (right side up). Baste with safety pins every 4-5 inches. Quilt with simple straight lines, a gentle stipple, or tie with yarn at block corners. Trim edges even and bind with 2 1/2" strips.

warnings.message: Wash baby quilts before gifting to soften and pre-shrink. Use a gentle detergent.

tips.text: Tying is the fastest finish — use embroidery floss or baby yarn at each block intersection.
Flannel backing is extra cozy for baby quilts and easier to baste because it grips.
